discovery.run_summary: Strong risk-on tape led by semis (SMH +5.8%, TSM +6.9%, AVGO +4.7%) on what appears to be a broad chip rally tied to Trump's Intel/Nvidia/Apple semiconductor manufacturing narrative. VIX collapsed 11% to 16.4 as Iran ceasefire/oil-down narrative dominates. Energy and financials lag; mid-cap semi-equipment names (ONTO, VECO, AEIS) and rate-sensitive cyclicals participating in the rip.

notable_index_moves: SMH +5.76% and TSM +6.94% — semi rally appears tied to Trump Truth Social post about Intel/Nvidia/Apple/Elon TerraFab US chip buildout
VIX -11% to 16.4 on Iran peace/ceasefire narrative
XLE -1.65% as oil prices tumble on Strait of Hormuz reopening; gas below $4
DIA -0.15% vs QQQ +2.51% — sharp growth/value divergence
Kalshi traders pricing >50% odds of a Fed HIKE in 2026 on hawkish Warsh — yet stocks rallying anyway; tension worth watching

discoveries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
discoveries.setup: Highly oil-sensitive low-cost airline ripping on collapsing crude
discoveries.thesis: JBLU is a heavily shorted, fuel-cost-sensitive, balance-sheet-stressed airline. Combination of Iran ceasefire / oil down sharply + risk-on tape + low share price (high beta to good news) explains a double-digit move without any company-specific 8-K. Magnitude is large but consistent with the operating leverage from fuel cost relief on a thin-margin carrier.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued crude weakness; peer airlines (ALK, ULCC, SAVE) showing similar sized moves; no JBLU-specific filing materializes in next 48h
discoveries.what_kills: An 8-K or news showing capital raise, covenant issue, or strategic transaction would change the read entirely; oil reversing hard on Iran deal breakdown
discoveries.what_to_learn: Sub-$10 airlines with high fuel sensitivity routinely produce 10%+ days on macro oil moves alone — don't fade these as 'overdone' without checking the crude tape first
discoveries.catalyst: Oil collapse on Iran ceasefire narrative + risk-on broad tape
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals, but Trump posts and CNBC headlines all confirm oil-down/Iran-peace as dominant macro driver today
research_pointers: Compare JBLU move to ALK, LUV, AAL, ULCC, SAVE to confirm sector-wide rather than idiosyncratic
Check JBLU fuel hedge book — unhedged carriers benefit most
Short interest data; JBLU has historically had heavy short interest that amplifies macro rallies

discoveries.name: Pool Corporation
discoveries.setup: Distributor with 5x normal volume but only moderate price move and no catalyst signals
discoveries.thesis: Volume multiple of 5.2x on a ~2.4% move with no 8-K, no earnings, and no obvious sector catalyst is genuinely unusual. Could be analyst action, institutional rebalance, or quiet news flow we can't see — cannot construct a confident bull or bear case from available evidence.
discoveries.what_confirms: Discovery of an analyst upgrade, M&A rumor, or insider filing would explain it
discoveries.what_kills: If volume turns out to be options-expiry related or index rebalance flow, the signal is noise
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — high volume but no filing or news visible
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty; the 5.23x volume multiple is the only flag
research_pointers: Search for POOL analyst notes today
Check if today is a quad-witching or index rebalance date that could explain volume
Check housing/pool industry peer moves (LESL, HAYW)

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-equipment mid-cap riding TSM/SMH-led semiconductor melt-up
discoveries.thesis: ONTO move is fully explained by SMH +5.76% and TSM +6.94%. As a semi-process-control name, it is high-beta to the equipment sub-sector and to the Trump 'America builds chips' narrative (TerraFab, Intel/Nvidia/Apple). Magnitude is in line with sector, not in excess of it.
discoveries.what_confirms: Move proportional to peers KLAC, CAMT, NVMI, AEIS, VECO; no idiosyncratic 8-K appears
discoveries.what_kills: A specific Onto announcement (customer loss, guidance cut) appearing would shift interpretation
discoveries.catalyst: Semi sector rally + Trump semiconductor onshoring narrative
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Trump post explicitly names Intel, Nvidia, Apple, and Elon's TerraFab as US chip buildout participants — drives equipment names
research_pointers: Confirm move is in-line with KLAC, AMAT, LRCX rather than outsized
Track whether semi-cap-equipment basket can hold these gains if Fed hike odds keep climbing
Check ONTO exposure to advanced packaging — the part of the buildout most directly hit by Trump's narrative

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
discoveries.setup: Semi-power-supply name lifted by sector rally; benign 8-K filed last week
discoveries.thesis: AEIS is power conversion for semi-cap equipment — direct beneficiary of the chip rally. The 8-K from June 12 had only 8.01 + 9.01 items (catch-all + boilerplate), which are not severity indicators on their own. Move appears to be sector beta, not idiosyncratic.
discoveries.what_confirms: Move tracks SMH and semi-equipment peers; no follow-on filing in next 5 days
discoveries.what_kills: If the 8.01 turned out to be a guidance pre-announcement (we can't read the URL contents from here), the move could be idiosyncratic and have more legs
discoveries.catalyst: Semi rally; possible residual effect of 6/12 8-K
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K has only catch-all and boilerplate items; cannot infer severity without reading the exhibit
research_pointers: Pull the 6/12 8-K exhibit to see what the 8.01 event was — could be conference presentation guidance
Compare AEIS to MKSI as power-conversion peer

discoveries.name: Align Technology
discoveries.setup: Beaten-down consumer-healthcare name rallying against a weak XLV tape
discoveries.thesis: ALGN up ~5% on a day XLV is down 0.87% is a notable relative-strength signal — the name is going up against its sector tape, with elevated volume (1.76x). No 8-K and no earnings, but the combination of risk-on + IWM strength + consumer discretionary +1.45% suggests ALGN is being re-rated as a discretionary recovery name. If risk-on persists, a beaten-down consumer-medtech can keep grinding higher as shorts cover.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued outperformance vs XLV in coming sessions; analyst upgrade on consumer recovery; positive read-through from any consumer discretionary peer guide-up
discoveries.what_kills: Reversal of risk-on tape; XLV continues to bleed and ALGN follows; an 8-K with adverse content appears
discoveries.what_to_learn: Counter-tape moves (stock up while sector is down) with elevated volume and no visible catalyst are often early signs of positioning shift — worth flagging even without a named catalyst
discoveries.catalyst: Apparent positioning shift / consumer discretionary risk-on; no specific filing
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; signal is the relative strength vs XLV combined with above-avg volume
research_pointers: Check Invisalign monthly volume data / dental industry KOL channel checks
Look for analyst upgrades or short interest data on ALGN
Compare to other beaten-down consumer-medtech (DXCM, NVRO)

discoveries.name: Beam Therapeutics
discoveries.setup: Speculative biotech up 16% over 5 days with no visible catalyst
discoveries.thesis: Beam has run hard over 5 days into today's continuation. XBI is only +0.95%, so this is outperforming the biotech tape. Could be conference data anticipation, M&A speculation, or a pipeline readout — cannot identify which from available evidence. Genuinely cannot articulate either side at conf 3+.
discoveries.what_confirms: n/a — UNCLEAR by genuine absence of information
research_pointers: Check biotech conference calendar (ASH, ASCO follow-ups, gene-editing conferences) for BEAM presentations
Verkko / CRSP / NTLA peer moves — gene editing sympathy?
Check options flow / unusual call activity

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Trump Truth Social post explicitly naming Intel/Nvidia/Apple/Elon TerraFab partnership for US semiconductor manufacturing
Iran ceasefire / oil collapse / Strait of Hormuz reopening

secondary_opportunities.relationship: negative — energy names hurt by crude weakness

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump details US semiconductor buildout: Intel + Nvidia partnership, Elon's TerraFab, Apple manufacturing in US, with US government holding 10% of Intel
trump_signals.note: This appears to be a major driver of today's semi rip (SMH +5.76%). TSM up 6.94% is interesting — narrative is US onshoring AWAY from Taiwan yet TSM rallies, likely because TSM's Arizona fabs are integral to the buildout. Watch for sympathy in semi-cap-equipment mid-caps. The Trump claim that government's Intel stake is now worth $60B is itself market-moving rhetoric.

trump_signals.post_summary: Repeated posts emphasizing oil prices down, stocks at records, Iran peace deal holding
trump_signals.note: Trump is actively talking down oil — combined with Navy ending Iran blockade, this is a sustained policy push for low crude. Energy names face continued headwind; fuel-sensitive cyclicals (airlines, trucking) get tailwind.

ai_announcements.headline: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips
ai_announcements.summary: AWS is in talks to sell its Trainium/Inferentia chips to other data centers, framed by Jassy as a $50B opportunity. This is a direct frontal move at Nvidia's merchant silicon TAM.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: More AI silicon suppliers (AWS competing with NVDA) means MORE wafer starts and more advanced packaging metrology demand. Onto benefits from any expansion of AI chip production regardless of which vendor wins. Also tied to HBM/advanced packaging which scales with total AI compute, not vendor share.
research_pointers: Onto's exposure to HBM inspection and advanced packaging tools
Whether AWS Trainium production at TSMC/Samsung uses Onto metrology
Semi equipment rally today may be broader (VECO, AEIS, ONTO all up 4-5%) suggesting sector flow, not single-name

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Veeco's MOCVD/laser annealing/ion beam tools serve advanced node and packaging. More AI chip competition = more total fab capex. Beneficiary of TAM expansion.
research_pointers: Sector-wide semi-cap move today (ONTO, AEIS, VECO all up ~5%) suggests broad bid, possibly tied to FERC grid fast-lane news + AWS chip story
Check if VECO has direct AWS/TSMC exposure for Trainium nodes

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Advanced Energy supplies power conversion for semi equipment and data centers. FERC grid fast-lane + AWS chip TAM = double tailwind. AEIS also had an 8-K on 6/12 (Item 8.01 'other events') which could be the actual catalyst, not AI news.
research_pointers: Read the 6/12 8-K Item 8.01 — likely the real driver, not today's AI news
Data center power exposure makes FERC fast-lane interconnect news a direct positive

ai_announcements.headline: FERC mandates fast-lane grid interconnection for AI data centers
ai_announcements.summary: FERC ordered grid operators to prioritize data center interconnections but did NOT solve underlying supply. Net positive for data center buildout pace; neutral-to-negative for grid capacity strain.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Power conversion / electrical equipment plays directly benefit from faster data center buildout timelines.
research_pointers: Confirm whether AEIS data center revenue mix has been growing
Cross-reference with peer moves (VRT, ETN, GEV) to validate FERC catalyst attribution

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most movers in the market_data list (JBLU, POOL, VCYT, ALGN, BEAM, BLDR, WSO) have no plausible link to today's AI announcements — these are likely sector/idiosyncratic moves (e.g., POOL 5x volume + WSO move suggests industrial distribution-specific news; JBLU +10% is airline-specific; ALGN/BEAM/VCYT healthcare-specific). Semi-equipment cluster (ONTO/VECO/AEIS) is the only group with a coherent AI-news linkage today via AWS-chips-TAM + FERC grid fast-lane. No Anthropic news in feed.
